feed.title: blank
feed.subtitle: A simple, whitespace theme for academics. Based on [*folio](https://github.com/bogoli/-folio) design.

entry.title: DIMVA’24
entry.content:
DIMVA’24 took place this weekend, where I had the pleasure of presenting my poster showcasing the progress we have made in the development of the RAMPAGE framework. With this work, we aim to address one of the most significant shortcomings we have found in the literature on DGA detection: the reproducibility of experiments and models.

The conference was held at EPFL in Lausanne, on the shores of Lake Geneva. In addition to enjoying indescribable views, on the last day, we shared a barbecue dinner by the lake, enjoying the beach and a very summery atmosphere (despite being many kilometers from the sea). It was undoubtedly something unforgettable.

It is always a pleasure to participate in academic events of this kind, as they are perfect opportunities to meet other researchers from different countries around the world, with interesting stories and life experiences. There was not a single talk that didn’t make the trip to Lausanne worthwhile.

I look forward to seeing both new and familiar faces at DIMVA’25!

entry.title: GSEC 2024
entry.content:
Today, the reversea.me team had the pleasure of visiting the GSEC (Google Safety Engineering Center) offices in Málaga, where we had the opportunity to meet and exchange words with the VirusTotal development team in person.

During the visit, in addition to touring their facilities with sea views, we had a productive meeting where we presented our research directions to establish synergies between teams. It was a fruitful meeting that marked the beginning of a promising future collaboration between the university and Google. We are in touch!

Per aspera ad astra, but always remember that memento mori.
entry.summary: Visit to the GSEC in Málaga 2024

entry.title: JNIC24
entry.content:
I had the privilege of attending the JNIC (Jornadas Nacionales de Investigación en Ciberseguridad). There, I was able to attend various talks where different research projects currently being conducted in Spain regarding cybersecurity were presented.

In addition to cybersecurity, we were able to do some networking with different researchers from other universities in the country, such as those from Castilla-La Mancha and Galicia.

Next year, we will be back with even more enthusiasm.
